fitScoreNote: I've spent the last two years as the technical voice in every enterprise deal my companies ran, and I've been building on the Notion API in my own work long enough that I can prototype solutions in the field rather than promising to follow up. This is the SE motion I want to do well, and Notion is the product I'd be proud to sell.

coreJobFunction.rationale: Dan's career at Smarter Technologies as Director of Sales Engineering is nearly identical to this role: he ran pre-sales technical discovery, built and delivered custom prospect-specific demos, managed 61 enterprise deals end-to-end, conducted architecture walkthroughs with CIOs and IT security teams, and achieved a 96% prospect-to-proposal conversion rate. His ability to translate complex AI and technical concepts to non-technical executives is extensively documented and was literally the reason he survived an 85% RIF as the only non-Product/Engineering person retained, then was promoted to VP of Solutions within his first month. His Solutions Architect work at Thoughtful adds further direct overlap with POC development, customer requirements gathering, API integration scoping, and cross-functional deal enablement. The minor gap is that this role is for a productivity/collaboration SaaS product (Notion) rather than enterprise healthcare automation, meaning the domain shifts, but the SE motion, demo engineering, stakeholder management, and GTM collaboration are near-identical.

seniority.rationale: Dan has ~8 years of post-college experience (FanDuel 2016-2019, Google 2020-2021, Action Network 2021-2022, Thoughtful AI 2023-2025, Smarter Technologies 2025-present) with a clear title arc from analyst to Solutions Architect to Customer Engineer to Lead TPM to Director of Sales Engineering to VP of Solutions. His SE work is directly analogous to the Notion role: he owned 61 enterprise deals end-to-end under $31.8M in pipeline, built and ran a full demo engineering platform with prospect-specific custom demos, led technical discovery and architecture presentations with CIOs and IT security leaders, and managed API/integration scoping across 12+ EMR platforms. He is a strong individual contributor with team-building and cross-functional coordination experience (working alongside CEOs, VPs of Sales, Delivery, and Product) rather than a large people-manager, which is appropriate for a Solutions Engineer IC role. The one minor gap is that his SE experience is entirely in healthcare RCM rather than productivity/collaboration software, but the presales motion, technical depth, enterprise stakeholder management, and demo engineering skills are a near-exact match.

technicalSkills.rationale: Dan has strong, production-level evidence for the core SE skill set this role demands: he managed 61 enterprise deals end-to-end, built a full demo engineering platform (20+ reusable workflows), led technical discovery with health system CIOs, and drove $1.95M in closed-won revenue as Director of Sales Engineering. His API integration depth is genuine (Notion API, Fireflies GraphQL, Google Drive/Sheets OAuth2, FHIR, RPA), and he is a self-described Notion power user who served as workspace admin at Thoughtful AI and built production integrations against the Notion API. He also has extensive JavaScript and frontend experience: the personal website is a production Next.js/TypeScript/React application with streaming APIs and agentic tool loops, and he has shipped DocWow and QuickSight portal integrations in Next.js as well. The primary gap is that his enterprise SE experience is entirely in healthcare/RCM, not horizontal SaaS productivity tooling, which is Notion's context; translating that domain expertise to a general-purpose enterprise software sale is a modest but real adjustment.

industryVertical.rationale: Notion is an explicitly horizontal productivity and collaboration platform with no preferred industry vertical: its ICP spans enterprise ops, GTM, product, and engineering teams across all industries (customers include Toyota, Figma, and OpenAI). Because the platform is horizontal, domain-specific expertise matters less here than for a vertical SaaS SE role. Dan's core enterprise SE skills, technical discovery framework, stakeholder management, and demo engineering ability transfer directly regardless of vertical. He also brings genuine Notion-specific depth that most candidates lack: he served as workspace admin at Thoughtful AI building structured linked databases, rollup formulas, and permission hierarchies for a cross-functional team; he is a self-described power user who has rebuilt his personal operating system in Notion; and he has built production integrations against the Notion API. The modest scoring deduction reflects that his prior deal experience is exclusively in healthcare RCM personas rather than the broad cross-industry enterprise buyer base Notion serves, but the SE motion itself is a near-exact match and his Notion depth is a direct compensating signal.

logistics.rationale: Dan is a deeply rooted San Francisco resident (Richmond neighborhood since 2022, engaged, wedding planned in California) with zero relocation friction for Notion's SF-headquartered role. His enterprise travel history, including on-site workshops at Jefferson Health and Presbyterian Health in New Mexico, confirms travel tolerance. The JD did not render a compensation band, but Notion SE AMER roles typically carry OTE in the $200K-$280K range, consistent with director/senior IC market rates. The only minor friction is the slight downward step from his most recent Director of Sales Engineering and VP of Solutions titles into an individual contributor SE role, but that is a seniority/title question rather than a logistical one.

summary: Managed 61 enterprise deals totaling $31.8M pipeline with a 96% prospect-to-proposal conversion rate.
Built a demo engineering platform that compressed prospect-specific demo creation from days to hours.
Retained as the only non-Product/Engineering person in an 85% company reduction, promoted to VP of Solutions within the first month.
Production Notion API builder and longtime workspace admin who can demo and prototype natively on day one.
Translated complex AI architecture into business outcomes for C-suite audiences across 12 EMR platforms.

strengths.description: I've run the full enterprise SE motion end-to-end, managing 61 deals totaling $31.8M in pipeline with a 96% prospect-to-proposal conversion rate.

strengths.description: I built a full demo engineering platform from scratch that reduced prospect-specific demo creation from 3-4 days to 3-4 hours, with automated validation before every CIO-level presentation.

strengths.description: I'm a genuine Notion power user and former workspace admin at Thoughtful AI who has built production integrations against the Notion API. I'm not just selling the product, I've built with it.

strengths.description: Retained as the only non-Product/Engineering person through an 85% company reduction, then promoted to VP of Solutions at AccessHealthcare within my first month. That outcome is the clearest signal I have about what I actually do well: translating complex technical systems into language that moves skeptical enterprise buyers from uncertain to convinced.

strengths.description: I have extensive JavaScript and frontend experience alongside deep backend and API integration depth. This personal website is a production Next.js/TypeScript/React application with streaming APIs, agentic tool loops, and Clerk auth. I've also shipped DocWow in Next.js and built QuickSight dashboards embedded into Next.js customer portals at Thoughtful.

strengths.title: I've done the cross-functional GTM coordination this role requires, working along
strengths.description: I've done the cross-functional GTM coordination this role requires, working alongside CEOs, VPs of Sales, and Product teams to turn pre-sales discovery into tighter scoping and faster closes.

weaknesses.title: My enterprise SE experience has been exclusively in healthcare RCM, not in a hor
weaknesses.description: My enterprise SE experience has been exclusively in healthcare RCM, not in a horizontal SaaS product like Notion where the ICP spans ops, GTM, product, and engineering teams across every industry.
weaknesses.mitigation: The SE motion, discovery, technical feasibility, demo engineering, stakeholder management, is the same muscle regardless of domain. I've sold AI automation to skeptical CIOs and billing teams; helping an enterprise ops or GTM leader understand how to consolidate their team's workflow into Notion is a shorter translation than that. And unlike most candidates, I come in already having built production systems on the Notion API and having served as workspace admin, so I'm not learning the product in the field.

weaknesses.title: My Salesforce experience is limited to using it as a reporting layer and data sy
weaknesses.description: My Salesforce experience is limited to using it as a reporting layer and data sync target. I've built custom reports and dashboards for executive visibility, but I haven't been a Salesforce admin, built Salesforce workflows, or integrated it via the Salesforce API at depth.
weaknesses.mitigation: I'd be direct about this gap and lean on my quick ramp track record. I built the Notion API integration platform from zero in under a month while simultaneously running 61 live enterprise deals. Salesforce's data model and API are well-documented; I'm confident I'd cover the learning curve quickly and wouldn't be blocked on it in the field.

weaknesses.title: My most recent titles (Director of Sales Engineering and VP of Solutions) are se
weaknesses.description: My most recent titles (Director of Sales Engineering and VP of Solutions) are senior to a standard IC Solutions Engineer role, which could raise questions about whether I'll stay engaged.
weaknesses.mitigation: Those titles reflected scope at a startup and a transitional PE situation, not a big company org chart. What I'm looking for is the right SE motion at the right company: deep enterprise deals, technical complexity, a product I genuinely believe in. Notion is that company. I'm not here because I've run out of options; I'm here because this is the role I want to do well.

referralBlurb: Dan ran the full enterprise SE motion at our company: built the demo engineering platform, led CIO-level architecture walkthroughs, and converted 96% of prospects to proposals across $31.8M in pipeline. He was the only non-Product/Engineering person kept when our company reduced by 85%, then moved directly into a VP of Solutions role at AccessHealthcare within his first month. What makes him unusual for a Notion SE role is that he's also a genuine power user who served as workspace admin and has built production integrations against the Notion API, so he's not learning the product on the job. He's exactly the kind of technical seller who can walk into a complex enterprise deal and credibly own both the discovery and the demo.

projects.name: Notion Meeting Intelligence
projects.relevance: A multi-agent pipeline that writes structured deal intelligence directly into Notion, demonstrating both GTM AI building and deep Notion-native development - exactly the kind of production work that signals genuine platform depth rather than surface-level familiarity.

projects.name: Personal Website Agent
projects.relevance: An agent-first site built on the Claude Agent SDK with live career search, job description analysis, and meeting scheduling. Shows full-stack production engineering and the kind of agentic systems thinking that enterprise prospects increasingly want to understand from their SE.

solutioningDecks.title: Arcline EPD Weekly Digest
solutioningDecks.description: Full walkthrough of the EPD solution built for Arcline Technologies to automatically create weekly readouts for VP-Level stakeholders. Truthfulness of the summarized claims is checked by humans and AI alike to ensure nothing is missed or hallucinated.
